title: platform-user — product analytics
description: Analytics events on the Stackra-staff plane. These land in the internal analytics tenant (a separate Segment source from the tenant-facing product), never mixed with tenant end-user events. Consent tier is `essential` — platform-staff analytics are internal-workforce metrics governed by the employment contract, not by the tenant-plane consent framework.

events.name: Platform User Hired
events.description: New Stackra staffer provisioned. Feeds the headcount + hiring-velocity dashboards.

events.name: Platform User Offboarded
events.description: Stackra staffer offboarded. `trigger` distinguishes voluntary vs involuntary. Feeds attrition dashboards.

events.name: Platform User Department Changed
events.description: Internal transfer. Feeds the internal-mobility dashboard.

events.name: Platform User Manager Reassigned
events.description: Org-chart mutation. Spikes correlate with re-org events.

events.name: Platform User Suspended
events.description: Temporary suspension. Reason not tracked to analytics (HR-sensitive; stays in the audit trail).

events.name: Platform User MFA Enrolled
events.description: Consumed from identity::MfaEnrolled where the referenced Identity is linked to a PlatformUser — tracks staff-plane MFA-adoption independently of tenant MFA-adoption.

events.name: Platform User On Call Shift Started
events.description: Fired when OnCallResolver detects a new shift start (transitioned from no-current-shift to current-shift for a PlatformUser). Feeds the on-call rotation dashboard.

events.name: Platform User Access Revocation Completed
events.description: RevokePlatformUserAccessJob completed. `duration_seconds` is critical for the SOC 2 24h-SLA dashboard.

notes: There is deliberately NO 'Platform User PII Viewed' event to analytics — that stays in the audit trail (compliance evidence) and never leaves the Stackra boundary.
Segment write key resolves via env `SEGMENT_INTERNAL_WRITE_KEY` (distinct from `SEGMENT_TENANT_WRITE_KEY`).
The `actor_id` property is always a PlatformUser id (never a User id). Cross-plane actor references are not permitted — platform events are staff-plane only.
